---
title: Homemade pasta dough
date: 2024-02-03
tags: [cooking, italian, pasta]
---

The classic ratio is one egg per hundred grams of flour, with a pinch of salt and a splash of olive oil. I use tipo 00 for silky sheets and semola rimacinata when I want a little more bite. Everything goes onto the counter in a well; the eggs are whisked in gradually with a fork until the dough pulls together.

Kneading is where patience pays off. Ten minutes of steady work turns a rough ball into something smooth and elastic that springs back when poked. I wrap it tightly and rest it for at least half an hour so the gluten relaxes before rolling.

Rolling thin enough to read a newspaper through is the traditional test. I cut tagliatelle by hand with a long knife, dust the ribbons with semola, and drop them into heavily salted boiling water for ninety seconds. Fresh pasta is forgiving of rustic sauces but unforgiving of overcooking.
